title: pH Probe BNC Amplifier Interface
design_question: How to condition a high-impedance pH electrode output (50–500 MΩ) for reading by an Arduino ADC without loading the electrode?
selected_approach: DFRobot SEN0161 pH meter module (or equivalent). BNC connector for standard pH probe; on-board AD8603 instrumentation amplifier with 100 TΩ input impedance. Output: 0–3.3 V linear, centred at 2.5 V for pH 7. MCU reads output on 10-bit ADC. 2-point calibration (pH 4 and pH 7 buffer powders dissolved in distilled water).
rationale: pH electrode output impedance (50–500 MΩ) exceeds Arduino ADC input impedance (100 kΩ) by 1000×, causing severe loading error. Dedicated pH module with instrumentation amplifier eliminates this. DFRobot SEN0161 is widely supported, < £10, and provides a calibrated 0–3.3 V output.

alternatives.reason_rejected: More accurate (±0.002 pH) and has serial interface, but costs £60 — overkill for educational or hobbyist water monitoring.
DIY follower circuit still requires precision components; calibration more involved; no protection for electrode.

verification_notes: FACT: 'pH Electrode — Calibration and Millivolt Measurement' confirms electrode impedance issue and 2-point calibration requirement.
supporting_fact_titles: pH Electrode — Calibration and Millivolt Measurement
